steps.title: Decode the logical requirement
steps.content: ”Necessary but not sufficient” means: every decrease in government size (total outlays) occurs when Congress is divided (necessary), but a divided Congress can exist without causing a decrease (not sufficient). The data must show both patterns: all reductions with divided Congresses, and at least one divided Congress with an increase instead.

steps.title: Match the data pattern
steps.content:
Choice D shows all divided Congresses except 1975–1976 with reductions in total outlays, while all undivided Congresses had increases. This proves necessity: all reductions occurred under divided Congresses. It also proves non-sufficiency: 1975–1976 was divided but increased. The other choices either focus on spending components (A, C) or incorrectly claim all divided Congresses produced reductions (B).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
